execution_rules: Do not make unsupported business-impact claims.
Do not use synthetic/demo data for model-performance claims unless labeled synthetic.
Do not leave active champion pointing to archived legacy artifacts.
Every ML change must include tests and model-card evidence.
Every user-facing metric must include provenance: measured, estimated, simulated, provisional, or unavailable.

tasks.title: Create CURRENT_STATE.md and CLAIMS.md
notes: Added root CURRENT_STATE.md and CLAIMS.md.
Updated README and legacy demo docs to point to root CLAIMS.md as the public claim boundary.
Synthetic/demo status is now called out explicitly in root truth docs.
actions: Summarize implemented, partial, planned, and do-not-claim capabilities.
Move public claim source of truth from docs/demo/CLAIMS_LEDGER.md into root CLAIMS.md.
Update README links.
acceptance: README links to CURRENT_STATE.md and CLAIMS.md.
No doc claims measured business impact without evidence.
Synthetic/demo status is explicit.

tasks.title: Archive internal/demo artifacts and stale model files
notes: Archived legacy demo docs under docs/archive/2026-04-legacy-demo and moved productization artifacts under docs/archive/productization_artifacts.
Removed dead frontend demo routes and welcome surfaces, and stopped the API client and debug auth flow from routing through demo-only tenant headers.

Archived Summit demo model artifacts under backend/models/archive/summit_demo, cleaned backend/models/registry.json to keep only the active champion, and moved stale one-off reports into backend/reports/archive.
Extracted production tenant setup into backend/scripts/production_tenant.py so setup_production no longer depends on the legacy demo seeding script.
actions: Archive demo-only docs and historical productization artifacts outside the active docs surface.
Remove dead frontend demo entry points and tenant-header routing.
Move stale Summit model artifacts and legacy report outputs into archive paths.
Extract live production setup responsibilities from legacy demo scripts.
acceptance: backend/models/champion.json does not point to archived legacy model.
README does not cite stale demo artifacts as evidence.
Archive README explains artifacts are not active.
Active routes and docs no longer depend on legacy demo surfaces.

tasks.title: Fix API namespace and tenant-doc mismatch
notes: Removed the duplicate experiments router include and normalized canonical examples to /api/v1/experiments.
Updated tenant/session naming references in technical docs.
Verified acceptance with pytest backend/tests/test_experiments_api.py -q and pytest backend/tests/test_security_guardrails.py -q.
actions: Remove duplicate experiments router registration.
Normalize experiments route under /api/v1/experiments.
Make tenant context naming consistent in code and docs.
acceptance: pytest backend/tests/test_experiments_api.py -q passes.
pytest backend/tests/test_security_guardrails.py -q passes.
Docs match actual session context variable.

phases.name: Focused benchmark evidence and model reset

tasks.title: Create focused data registry and remove active Favorita dependency
notes: Added DATA_SOURCES.md, data_registry metadata, and per-source README files for the active M5 + FreshRetailNet + CSV/Square scope.
Added a legacy Favorita note under docs/archive/data_sources/favorita.md and updated root CLAIMS.md and README.md.
Shifted training-validation and download script defaults away from Favorita-first behavior while keeping legacy/reference paths explicit.
actions: Declare M5/Walmart as the primary public benchmark.
Declare FreshRetailNet-50K as the secondary stockout/censored-demand benchmark.
Declare CSV + Square as the pilot/product validation path.
Declare Dominick's as deferred optional only.
Move Favorita out of the active champion path; keep it only as legacy/reference if needed.
Mark Rossmann, Instacart, Complete Journey, UCI Online Retail, and leaked/unauthorized data as out of active scope.
For every source, document allowed and disallowed claims.
acceptance: DATA_SOURCES.md clearly states that the active build uses M5 + FreshRetailNet + CSV/Square only.
No active model champion metadata names Favorita.
Root CLAIMS.md distinguishes benchmark evidence from pilot evidence.
No task requires unauthorized, leaked, or legally ambiguous data.

tasks.title: Add dataset snapshot infrastructure
notes: Added backend/ml/dataset_snapshots.py with deterministic hashing, provenance metadata, and snapshot persistence.
Added the dataset_snapshots DB model and migration, and threaded dataset_snapshot_id into saved model metadata.
Updated run_training.py, run_onboarding_flow.py, and workers/retrain.py to create and persist snapshots during training flows.
actions: Persist dataset id, source, row count, stores, products, date range, hash, schema version, frequency, grain, geography, and implementation status.
Attach dataset_snapshot_id to model metadata.
Support M5, FreshRetailNet, CSV, and Square tenant snapshots.
Attach dataset claim boundaries or a pointer to data_registry/datasets.yaml.
acceptance: Dataset snapshot ID is returned by training scripts.
Snapshot metadata appears in model metadata.
Test validates stable hash for fixed fixture.
Snapshot includes source provenance and claim boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement M5/Walmart adapter and canonicalization
notes: Added backend/data_sources/m5.py and backend/scripts/prepare_m5.py for M5 canonicalization.
Updated ml.data_contracts to recognize M5 directory layouts and preserve extra benchmark fields such as price and calendar events.
Added adapter tests and made benchmark_datasets.py accept an explicit dataset/data-dir path so prepared M5 data can be benchmarked.
actions: Convert M5 calendar, sell prices, and sales tables into canonical ShelfOps store-product-day records.
Preserve hierarchy fields: item, department, category, store, state, and aggregate IDs where available.
Attach sell price and calendar event features.
Create deterministic dataset snapshot.
Add a small fixture for tests; do not commit the full dataset if large or license-sensitive.
acceptance: Adapter emits canonical rows with store_id, product_id, date, units_sold, price, category hierarchy, and calendar/event fields.
Adapter does not create fake inventory, PO, or supplier data without simulated provenance labels.
M5 snapshot can be used by benchmark script.

tasks.title: Replace XGBoost benchmark with LightGBM plus baselines on M5
notes: Added backend/ml/baselines.py with naive, seasonal naive, moving average, category/store average, and intermittent-demand baselines.
Rewrote backend/scripts/benchmark_datasets.py around a LightGBM benchmark path with JSON and Markdown output and no XGBoost dependency.
Marked M5 business-impact fields as not_available in the benchmark report and verified the script is import-safe enough for --help.
actions: Implement naive, seasonal naive, moving average, category/store average, and intermittent-demand baselines.
Run current LightGBM training path in benchmark script.
Make M5 the default benchmark dataset.
Output baseline comparison JSON and Markdown.
acceptance: Benchmark report includes each baseline and LightGBM.
Report includes WAPE, MASE, MAE, bias, interval metrics if available, rows, date ranges, and segment coverage.
Stockout, PO, and supplier metrics are labeled not_available for M5 unless generated by an explicitly labeled simulation.
No XGBoost dependency is required for the current benchmark path.

tasks.title: Add segment metrics for M5 and FreshRetailNet
notes: Added backend/ml/segments.py and backend/ml/evaluation.py for global plus segment metric reporting.
Implemented fast, medium, slow, intermittent, cold-start, promoted, high-volume, perishable, and stockout-window segment handling.
Added low-sample labeling and missing-field-safe evaluation tests.
actions: Segment SKUs into fast, medium, slow, intermittent, cold-start, promoted, and high-volume categories where possible.
For M5, use hierarchy and demand-frequency based segments.
For FreshRetailNet, additionally support perishable and stockout-window segments.
Compute global and segment metrics with low-sample labels.
acceptance: Evaluation report has global and segment metrics.
Low sample segments are labeled.
Empty or missing segment fields do not crash evaluation.

tasks.title: Add calibrated prediction intervals
notes: Added calibration and quantile helper modules plus prediction-path interval metadata fields.
predict_demand now returns interval_method, calibration_status, and interval_coverage while explicitly labeling heuristic intervals as uncalibrated.
Training now writes split-conformal interval metadata, coverage, and conformal residual quantiles into saved model artifacts.
actions: Implement LightGBM quantile p10/p50/p90 or split-conformal intervals.
Store interval method and coverage in metadata.
Return calibration status from prediction path.
Downgrade any heuristic interval language to uncalibrated.
acceptance: predict_demand returns intervals with interval_method and calibration_status.
Model card reports interval coverage.
If intervals are heuristic, UI badge says uncalibrated.

tasks.title: Generate new active M5 champion and model card
notes: Downloaded the standard M5 files into data/benchmarks/m5_walmart/raw from a public mirror after Kaggle competition auth was unavailable locally.

Added reproducible subset preparation to backend/scripts/prepare_m5.py and generated data/benchmarks/m5_walmart/subset_20spc with 600 balanced store/category series, 1,147,800 canonical rows, and dataset snapshot dsnap_09e19c9147a57fe5.
Benchmarked the subset in backend/reports/m5_subset20_benchmark.json and recorded the promotion holdout check in backend/reports/m5_subset20_holdout_eval.json.

Trained v3 on the M5 subset, promoted it to champion, updated backend/models/champion.json and backend/models/registry.json, and wrote MODEL_CARD.md with explicit benchmark claim boundaries and M5 limitations.
actions: Train current LightGBM on the M5/Walmart benchmark.
Use enough rows and enough time coverage to be credible.
Include dataset snapshot ID.
Archive old v1 champion.
Promote only if baselines and gates pass.
Label M5 limitations: no live inventory, no PO decisions, no supplier lead-time history, no merchant ROI.
acceptance: No active champion has rows_trained: 27.
Active metadata uses lightgbm, not XGBoost/LSTM weights.
Active dataset ID is m5_walmart or equivalent, not Favorita.
MODEL_CARD.md exists and includes limitations.

tasks.title: Add FreshRetailNet stockout/censored-demand track
actions: Convert FreshRetailNet into canonical ShelfOps records with hourly/daily handling explicitly documented.
Preserve stockout status, promotions, precipitation, perishability/category fields, and temporal fields.
Add metrics for stockout-window bias, non-stockout-window error, under-forecast rate during stockouts, and recovered-demand comparison if implemented.
Add a clearly labeled latent-demand recovery experiment or conservative censored-demand adjustment.
Keep this as a secondary evidence track, not the active M5 champion unless explicitly selected.
acceptance: FreshRetailNet benchmark report separates stockout and non-stockout periods.
Stockout-aware report says the dataset is not U.S.-based and does not prove merchant ROI.
UI/model evidence can show stockout-aware methodology without blending it into M5 metrics.
notes: Added backend/data_sources/freshretailnet.py and wired backend/ml/data_contracts.py to load train.parquet + eval.parquet as canonical FreshRetailNet records with stockout, promo, weather, and category fields preserved.

Added conservative stockout-aware recovery in backend/ml/latent_demand.py and evaluation metrics in backend/ml/stockout_metrics.py, with focused tests for adapter behavior, stockout metrics, dataset readiness, and snapshot hashing of array-like columns.

Downloaded FreshRetailNet-50K parquet files into data/benchmarks/freshretailnet_50k/raw from Hugging Face and generated backend/reports/freshretailnet_stockout_benchmark.json plus markdown on a deterministic 5000-series benchmark subset.
Wrote MODEL_CARD_STOCKOUT_APPENDIX.md to keep the FreshRetailNet methodology and claim boundaries separate from the active M5 champion story.

phases.goal: Replace legacy/demo model evidence with a focused M5/Walmart champion, add FreshRetailNet as a secondary stockout-aware evidence track, and document dataset claim boundaries.

tasks.title: Create recommendation service and policy layer
notes: Added recommendation schemas, forecast-window summarization, and a service that generates persisted replenishment recommendations from forecast, inventory, sourcing, and product cost context.
Recommendations now store forecast model version, policy version, interval method, calibration status, quantity, cost estimate, and risk labels in replenishment_recommendations.

Verified acceptance with pytest backend/tests/test_recommendation_service.py -q plus regression checks for backend/tests/test_inventory_optimizer.py, backend/tests/test_data_contracts.py, and backend/tests/test_ml_pipeline.py.
actions: Generate recommended quantity from forecast, current inventory, lead time, supplier constraints, and service level.
Store recommendation with model_version and policy_version.
Compute no-order stockout risk and order-overstock risk.
acceptance: Deterministic recommendation fixture passes.
Recommendation includes model version, horizon, interval method, lead time, quantity, cost, and risk labels.

tasks.title: Add replenishment API
notes: Added /api/v1/replenishment queue, detail, accept, edit, reject, and impact endpoints and registered the router in backend/api/main.py.
Accept and edit now create linked purchase_orders and po_decisions, while reject records explicit decision metadata on the recommendation record.
Verified acceptance with pytest backend/tests/test_replenishment_api.py -q and pytest backend/tests/test_purchase_orders_api.py -q.
actions: Add queue/detail/accept/edit/reject endpoints.
Record buyer decision with reason codes.
Link accepted recommendations to purchase orders.
acceptance: GET /api/v1/replenishment/queue returns buyer-ready cards.
Accept/edit/reject writes decision history.
Edited quantity requires reason code.

tasks.title: Add recommendation outcome computation
notes: Added recommendation_outcomes schema support plus async computation helpers in workers/monitoring.py to close the loop from recommendation to realized demand and inventory outcome.
Replenishment impact now summarizes closed vs provisional outcomes with explicit measured, estimated, and provisional confidence labels.
Verified acceptance with pytest backend/tests/test_recommendation_outcomes.py -q and broader regression over replenishment, purchase-order, inventory-policy, data-contract, and ML pipeline tests.
actions: After horizon closes, compute actual demand, stockout event, overstock event, forecast error, and estimated value.
Expose impact endpoint.
acceptance: Fixture can create recommendation, simulate actuals, and compute outcome.
Impact endpoint reports measured/estimated/provisional labels.

tasks.title: Build replenishment replay simulator
notes: Added a benchmark replay simulator comparing static, moving-average, seasonal-naive, and LightGBM-backed ShelfOps policy behavior over canonical benchmark transactions.
Simulation reports now label inventory, PO, lead-time, and cost assumptions as simulated and explicitly state that M5 replay is benchmark evidence, not measured merchant impact.

Verified with pytest backend/tests/test_replenishment_simulation.py backend/tests/test_data_contracts.py -q and generated backend/reports/replenishment_simulation_m5_subset20.json plus .md from the local M5 subset.
actions: Compare static reorder, moving average, seasonal naive, and ShelfOps model policy over M5 historical demand first.
Label inventory, PO, lead-time, and cost assumptions as simulated for M5.
Add FreshRetailNet stockout-aware replay only after the stockout track is implemented.
Output stockout days, lost-sales proxy, overstock units, overstock dollars, service level, PO count, and combined cost proxy.
acceptance: Report clearly separates benchmark simulation from measured merchant impact.
M5 simulation never claims true observed stockouts because M5 lacks live inventory/stockout status.
FreshRetailNet simulation uses explicit stockout-window labels when available.
Every metric has provenance: measured, benchmark, simulated, unavailable, or estimated.

tasks.title: Add simulation API
notes: Added /api/v1/simulations/replenishment to run or retrieve benchmark replay results for frontend/API consumption.
Simulation responses now include dataset snapshot, model version, policy version, and policy comparison rows instead of requiring direct artifact reads.

Verified with pytest backend/tests/test_replenishment_simulation.py backend/tests/test_simulations_api.py -q and regression on backend/tests/test_replenishment_api.py backend/tests/test_purchase_orders_api.py -q.
actions: Run or retrieve replenishment simulation results.
Return policy comparison table with dataset snapshot and policy/model versions.
acceptance: API returns simulation summary for frontend.
Results include dataset snapshot and policy versions.

phases.name: Integration hardening

tasks.title: Harden Square mapping/onboarding
notes: Added Square mapping preview and mapping confirmation endpoints, plus preview helpers that surface locations, products, variation ids, coverage, and unmapped external ids before sync.
Square sync now blocks production-style runs until mappings are confirmed, and demo id synthesis is only allowed for demo customers when explicitly enabled.
Sync health now exposes Square mapping coverage and unmapped ids, and README/CURRENT_STATE explicitly mark Shopify as deferred rather than partially implemented pilot scope.

Verified with pytest backend/tests/test_square_sync_mapping.py backend/tests/test_integrations_api.py backend/tests/test_contracts.py -q and regression on backend/tests/test_simulations_api.py backend/tests/test_replenishment_api.py -q.
actions: Add mapping preview for locations/products.
Require mapping confirmation before production sync.
Isolate demo ID synthesis behind demo-only settings.
Record unmapped external IDs.
Do not add Shopify in this phase; document it as deferred.
acceptance: Mapping preview displays Square locations/products/variations before sync.
Demo ID synthesis is blocked outside demo mode.
Unmapped IDs appear in data readiness.
Shopify is documented as deferred, not partially implemented.

tasks.title: Add webhook event log and replay
notes: Added webhook_event_log schema support and persist-before-processing behavior for Square webhooks, including signature failures, retry attempts, and dead-letter status.
Added authenticated replay and dead-letter list endpoints so failed webhook deliveries can be retried and inspected through the API.
Verified with pytest backend/tests/test_webhook_replay.py backend/tests/test_integrations_api.py backend/tests/test_contracts.py -q and regression on backend/tests/test_square_sync_mapping.py -q.
actions: Persist inbound webhook payloads before processing.
Add status/retry/dead-letter flow.
Add replay endpoint or script.
acceptance: Webhook stored before processing.
Failed webhook can be replayed.
Dead-letter events appear in operations API.

tasks.title: Add CSV onboarding
notes: Added backend/data_sources/csv_onboarding.py with validation, ingestion, and readiness evaluation for stores, products, transactions, and inventory CSVs.
Added authenticated CSV validate/ingest/readiness endpoints under /api/v1/data.
Verified with pytest backend/tests/test_csv_onboarding.py -q and pytest backend/tests/test_readiness_state_machine.py backend/tests/test_data_contracts.py backend/tests/test_integrations_api.py -q.
actions: Validate products, stores, transactions, and inventory CSVs.
Return actionable mapping errors.
Ingest only after validation.
acceptance: Bad CSV returns actionable errors.
Good CSV creates canonical records.
Data readiness updates after ingest.

phases.goal: Harden CSV and Square as the only active pilot ingestion paths; defer Shopify until the first pilot path is stable.

tasks.title: Build Replenishment Queue UI
notes: Added a replenishment route with queue filtering, search, detail drawer, and accept/edit/reject decision modal flows.
Surfaced interval provenance, risk badges, and impact provenance labels directly in the buyer workflow.
Verified with frontend npm run build.
actions: Add route and navigation item.
Show recommendation table, detail drawer, and accept/edit/reject modals.
Display uncertainty, data-quality, and provenance badges.
acceptance: User can act on a recommendation.
Edited quantity requires reason code.
Every risk/impact metric has provenance label.

tasks.title: Build Data Readiness UI
notes: Added a dedicated data-readiness route that explains trainability state, threshold progress, freshness, and Square mapping coverage.
Surfaced unmapped IDs and sync SLA breaches as explicit data-quality events instead of hiding them in backend-only responses.
Verified with frontend npm run build.
actions: Show trainability, recommendation eligibility, mapping coverage, and freshness.
acceptance: Page explains why tenant can/cannot train or generate recommendations.
Unmapped/stale/missing data is visible.

tasks.title: Build Pilot Impact UI
notes: Added a pilot-impact route that keeps operational outcome scorecards visually separate from benchmark replay evidence.
Added provenance badges so measured, estimated, provisional, and simulated metrics stay explicitly labeled in the UI.
Verified with frontend npm run build.
actions: Show forecast and business metrics.
Separate measured pilot metrics from simulated benchmark metrics.
acceptance: No metric appears without provenance label.
Simulation results are visually distinct from real pilot results.

tasks.title: Upgrade MLOps into Model Evidence UI
notes: Reworked the ML Ops page into a model-evidence surface centered on the active v3 champion, benchmark comparison, calibration, segment metrics, and claim boundaries.
Added explicit model-card, calibration, and segment panels backed by the repo's current champion artifacts and runtime effectiveness APIs.
Verified with frontend npm run build.
actions: Display champion model card, baseline comparison, calibration, segment metrics, and promotion-gate decision.
acceptance: Active champion evidence is visible.
Model limitations are visible.

tasks.title: Rewrite README and TECHNICAL docs
notes: Rewrote README around the replenishment decision loop, evidence boundaries, active data scope, and pilot packaging links.
Rewrote TECHNICAL.md around the actual runtime architecture and evidence stack instead of the older broad demo narrative.
actions: Center product loop and business evidence.
Move stack details below workflow.
Link model card, data sources, claims, and pilot playbook.
acceptance: README answers what decision ShelfOps improves and how it proves value.
No stale enterprise/demo overclaim.

tasks.title: Add pilot playbook and case-study template
notes: Added PILOT_PLAYBOOK.md defining pilot phases, required data, weekly cadence, success metrics, and evidence labeling rules.
Added docs/templates/CASE_STUDY_TEMPLATE.md to constrain future public pilot writeups to measured and correctly labeled outcomes.
actions: Define 6-8 week pilot process, data requirements, weekly cadence, and success metrics.
Create future case-study template with before/after or baseline-vs-policy comparison.
acceptance: A real merchant can understand required data and measured results.
Template prevents unsupported claims.

data_plan: Focused data plan: active scope is M5/Walmart as the primary public forecasting benchmark, FreshRetailNet-50K as the secondary stockout/censored-demand benchmark, and CSV + Square as the pilot/product validation path. Dominick's is deferred optional. Favorita, Rossmann, Instacart, Complete Journey, UCI Online Retail, and leaked/unauthorized data are out of active scope.
